items.title: Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Compromised in Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:

items.title: TrickMo Android banker adopts TON blockchain for covert comms
items.summary: A new variant of the TrickMo Android banking malware, delivered in campaigns targeting users across Europe, introduces new commands and uses The Open Network (TON) for stealthy command-and-control communications. [...]

items.title: Canvas System Is Online After a Cyberattack Disrupted Thousands of Schools
items.summary:
Tens of thousands of students studying for final exams around the world have regained access to a key online learning system after a cyberattack had earlier knocked it offline.
The post Canvas System Is Online After a Cyberattack Disrupted Thousands of Schools appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New ‘Dirty Frag’ Linux Vulnerability Possibly Exploited in Attacks
items.summary:
Also called Copy Fail 2 and tracked as CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500, the exploit was disclosed before a patch was released.
The post New ‘Dirty Frag’ Linux Vulnerability Possibly Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Resurrected ‘Crimenetwork’ Marketplace Taken Down, Administrator Arrested
items.summary:
The second iteration of the German-speaking online crime marketplace had over 22,000 users and more than 100 sellers.
The post Resurrected ‘Crimenetwork’ Marketplace Taken Down, Administrator Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Fake OpenAI Privacy Filter Repo Hits #1 on Hugging Face, Draws 244K Downloads
items.summary:
A malicious Hugging Face repository managed to take a spot in the platform's trending list by impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter open-weight model to deliver a Rust-based information stealer to Windows users.
The project, named Open-OSS/privacy-filter, masqueraded as its legitimate counterpart, released by OpenAI late last month (openai/privacy-filter), including copying the entire

items.title: Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign
items.summary:
Victims span across the aviation, critical infrastructure, energy, logistics, public administration, and technology sectors.
The post Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Quoting New York Times Editors’ Note
items.summary:
This article was updated after The Times learned that a remark attributed to Pierre Poilievre, the Conservative leader, was in fact an A.I.-generated summary of his views about Canadian politics that A.I. rendered as a quotation. The reporter should have checked the accuracy of what the A.I. tool returned.  The article now accurately quotes from a speech delivered by Mr. Poilievre in April. [...] He did not refer to politicians who changed allegiances as turncoats in that speech.
— New York Times Editors’ Note

    Tags: ai-ethics, hallucinations, generative-ai, new-york-times, journalis

items.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
